entry.title: Orbx Releases Oslo Gardermoen for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Orbx has released a new rendition of Oslo Gardermoen (ENGM) for MSFS 2024, developed by Finn Hansen and Jakub Lukaszewski. Across the airport, users can expect interior terminal modelling, updated photoreal ground textures, and 4K PBR materials, alongside a suite of custom ground equipment. Orbx also includes animated service traffic, covering pushback, buses, and other airside vehicles, and notes support for VDGS at applicable stands, which requires the Nool plugin. Oslo Gardermoen (ENGM) for MSFS 2024 is now available, priced at $21.23. MSFS 2020 customers can upgrade for free via Orbx Central. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Just Flight Publishes New A300B4 Development Update
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Just Flight has recently published a new development update (the first so far in 2026) on their rendition of the Airbus A300B4, showcasing some of the progress they have made, especially in the art department. While this development update may have been the first of the year, Just Flight has mentioned that the frequency of development updates will significantly increase from now on, as they are rapidly approaching the aircraft's beta testing phase. They have been putting a lot of effort into getting the lighting right by using video reference (hard to find since it’s such an old airplane), photos, FCOM information, and research trips to visit the real thing. In addition, they also simulated its many quirks and complexities, and spent many hours ensuring the intensity, color, and angles were correct. Furthermore, the A300B4 has some quirks that are pretty much unique to it, such as dedicated towing/park exterior navigation lights to help the ground crew, forward- and rear-facing navigation and strobe lights, separate wing and engine lights, and more. There is no release date or pricing information yet, but Threshold will keep you informed! If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates A350 Airliner for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has just released an update for their A350 Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024. This new update brings the aircraft to version 1.2.3. The primary focus of the update is on improving system stability, navigation logic, and overall usability. It also includes various other fixes, improvements, and changes. The update comes with a comprehensive changelog, which is detailed below: ‍ V1.2.3 SYSTEMS ⚙️ FIXED WASM crash after selecting STAR FIXED TCAS triggering from aircraft which are non factors FIXED Pitch jerkiness when passing through 10° with constant stick input FIXED Manually entered CRS shows in small font in the A350 FIXED TCAS returns all defined as "HEAVY" FIXED D-ATIS not timing out FIXED Inaccurate colours on SEC/PERF/APPR page FIXED OPT FL should be shown every 500ft FIXED Waypoint revision logic on Active, Temporary, and Wind pages FIXED Position/Report page formatting issues FIXED Flightplan STEP ALTs page logic/format FIXED Time marker should be in chronological order FIXED Font & sizing issues on PERF approach page FIXED Key input not masked anymore in simconnect FIXED Loadsheet formatting FIXED Flight Director sensitivity FIXED PDC response not being received from HOPPIE connections FIXED Dest ETA/ Wpt ETO not updating correctly (same time for multiple waypoints) FIXED Cost Index logic FIXED Letter "D" missing from ANF font FIXED HUD not displaying all the information all the time / Switch start state FIXED VD - missing magenta area FIXED Waypoint selection menu not showing for duplicates FIXED Inserting CMS by writing the waypoint name on ACTIVE/F-PLN/VERT REV/CMS page not working as expected. FIXED Moving thrust levers from CL to MCT detent & back to CL detent during CLB FMS now cancels derated thrust. FIXED Spoiler panel jitters rapidly while deployed in flight FIXED Misspelling waypoint in DATABASE WPTS page causes the page to go blank FIXED Constraints & altitudes below the TL be shown in feet instead of FLs FIXED ECAM AIR PACK 1(2) OFF fault error when performing packs off takeoff FIXED Inconsistencies in the display of planned speed on the CRZ/FPLN pages FIXED Trim not resetting correct direction after landing FIXED RA logic on touchdown FIXED PLACE DISTANCE WAYPOINT not working with negative distances FIXED "NONE" appearing in the middle of the procedure list instead of at the top FIXED SURV Controls - Switching the ADS-B RPTG should not switch the ADS-B TRAFFIC FIXED Inserting "1" as a CRZ FL on the ACTIVE/PERF/CRZ page should insert the current altitude as an CRZ altitude FIXED VD planned path incorrect given altitude restrictions. FIXED Sec airways inserting double airways when no intersection found FIXED RWY CONDITION / BRAKING not appearing on SD FIXED Step descent logic FIXED Return button behaviour on Step alt page FIXED ECAM COMPANY MSG memo for TELEX messages not clearing (internal only) FIXED Range Switching behaviour from ROSE/PLAN to ARC modes & vice versa FIXED Multiple amber arc indicating hottest wheel brake ‍ ART 🎨 FIXED Cabin windows flickering FIXED Cabin lighting turns off instead of dimming on "medium" & "dim" settings FIXED No difference between low & medium dim in cabin modes ‍ SOUND 🔊 FIXED RA callouts refined across all tracks/volume and attenuation balanced per community feedback FIXED Tie APU external sound to "external engines" volume slider FIXED Quiet exterior sounds in 2020 FIXED Refined gear extension sounds in cockpit and cabin when landing FIXED "Autobrake Off" callout not sounding after touchdown The latest update is now available to all current owners. If you do not own the aircraft, it can be purchased from the iniBuilds Store for approximately $80.37. ‍ For the latest flight simulation news, remember to stay tuned on Threshold! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: CFSS Releases Boundary Bay Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. CFSS has released a new rendition of Boundary Bay Airport for MSFS 2024, bringing one of Canada’s busiest general aviation airports and a major flight training hub into the sim. Located just south of Vancouver, CZBB is known more for business jets, turboprops, maintenance traffic, and training flights than airliner operations, and that focus is reflected in the scenery package. According to CFSS, the airport has been recreated using extensive research, with accurately modelled FBOs, hangars, terminal areas, aprons, and taxiways, all designed to reflect the real-world layout. The developer also says the scenery uses current charts for markings and lighting, which should make it particularly well-suited to regular GA flying.‍ The scenery requires MSFS 2024, along with CFSS Library V1.0.0 or later. You can purchase the scenery for USD $14.55 here. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Vector Confirms FSWeekend 2026 Plans, Shares New Systems Preview
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Vector has confirmed it will attend FSWeekend 2026 at the Lelystad Aviodrome on March 21 and 22, where the studio will further preview its recently announced Boeing 787 Dreamliner for MSFS 2024. For those unaware, Vector is a new name to the public flight simulation scene, having only recently broken silence and introduced itself. As part of the event, Vector will join a live Q&A session hosted by FSElite at 1030Z, on Sunday, March 22. According to the studio, the session will cover the team’s vision, offer more detail on the aircraft, and give the community a chance to ask questions directly. Alongside the announcement, Vector has shared a new work-in-progress image from the aircraft’s MAINT CTRL PGS page. The screenshot offers another early sign of the systems depth Vector is aiming for with Project Griffin, and continues the studio’s early messaging around a 787 built with a strong focus on fidelity, and operational realism. Vector notes that all data shown remains work in progress, and may change during development. With the studio only just beginning to introduce itself, FSWeekend will be the first real chance for many simmers to get a better sense of who Vector is, and what its 787 is aiming to deliver. Get your tickets here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Just Flight Publishes F70/F100 Development Update for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Just Flight has recently published a new development update concerning their rendition of the Fokker 70 and 100 for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, as they prepare builds of the two planes to be demonstrated at this weekend’s FSWeekend. The development update begins with an overview of the work done on the product as of late, with the VNAV system being one of the most important advances thus far, completely remade from scratch by their systems coder and delivering a fully autopilot-coupled VNAV. The previous one left them “unhappy” and “unsatisfied”, which led to the difficult and time-consuming decision to rewrite everything from scratch. Although it delayed the product’s launch quite significantly, it allowed them to incorporate their learnings from the first iteration into the base code, ultimately meaning both aircraft will be released with a very sophisticated system. They have made it so their VNAV logic can be easily tweaked and refined over the product’s lifetime. While the systems coder was hard at work on the VNAV system, the rest of their team continued refining other areas of the airplane, introducing new features never seen before in a Just Flight product. For starters, they have completed the integration of a performance calculator into their EFB, making use of real-world information to provide accurate take-off and landing calculations, taking things such as runway length, runway surface condition, aircraft systems state, and weather conditions, outputting take-off/landing distances, FLEX/TOGA thrust settings, and trim settings. The data can be input manually or imported from various sources via buttons located at the top of the calculator’s page. They have also expanded the list of map providers, and the visualization of SimBrief flight plans on the map is now a possibility. You can also refresh the METAR data of the origin/destination/alternate airport throughout the flight. ​ Furthermore, they have integrated the most comprehensive auto cabin crew simulation they have ever included in a product, featuring a large selection of pilot callouts across all phases of flight, as well as cockpit-to-cabin and cabin-to-cockpit interactions. There’s also a digital music player in the forward galley, which can be controlled manually or automatically by the cabin crew. ​ Alongside that, they have implemented a way to switch between digital music and announcements, something that would require a software swap via an SD card in real life, but they found a compromise by holding the Mode button for a while. ​ While switched into the announcement mode, the 13 included announcements can be manually played via the player’s own controls and/or automatically played by the cabin crew. The volume of announcements and music in the cabin will vary depending on the position of the cockpit door. Of course, PC users will be able to replace the files with their own custom versions, whether for music or announcements. ​ Both the cabin announcements and sounds are part of a sound set they now deem complete and will soon be showcased in a sound overview video. They have also provided a little teaser of what is to come: ​ Builds of both planes will be available to demo at the show throughout the full two days, at booth 31 in the Navigraph hall. ​If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: PMDG Updates 737 Lineup for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has recently issued an update to their MSFS 2024-compatible members of the 737 family (-600, -800, and 900), improving their WASM crash resistance. They have also provided a short development update on the new external model for the 737s and the upcoming update for the 777s. They have collected extensive data from their customers on WASM crashes to help mitigate them. So far, they have concluded that most of it stems from a certain instability in how the simulator allocates dynamic memory for third-party DLC in normal operation. A significant amount of time was invested in minimizing these incidents. They also found a flight path editing scenario that would often trigger late-in-flight WASM crashes, and added additional protections to ensure it doesn’t happen again. ‍ ​While PMDG doesn’t think they have completely won the battle against WASM crashes, the goal is to make them as rare as possible. As for the pending detail update for the 737 family, it is in final testing, and PMDG expects to release it by the end of the month. It will ship with the -700, and then be rolled out to the already released -600, -800, and -900. ​ They also have a 777 fleet update in the oven for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing WASM crash improvements and a bunch more that will be revealed closer to the release date. ​ PMDG’s 737 lineup is available on their website from $34.99 to $74.99, depending on the variant. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: iniBuilds Releases TriStar Preview Video, First Look Stream Now Live
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has released a new, in-depth preview video of its upcoming TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024, offering the most detailed look yet at the Lockheed L-1011 ahead of release. The newly published “Welcome to the TriStar” video runs for just about 50 minutes and provides a broad overview of both the aircraft itself and IniBuilds’ implementation, covering everything from modelling and operations to systems and flight model. You can watch it here. iniBuilds notes that the footage shown is from a work-in-progress version of the aircraft, meaning bugs may be present, and features may change before release. Alongside the video release, iniBuilds has also announced an official first look livestream, taking place at 1500Z on Microsoft’s Twitch channel. The TriStar Airliner is currently in development for MSFS 2024, with more previews and feature breakdowns expected in the lead-up to release. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Black Square Releases Cessna Caravan Professional for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Black Square has recently released their Cessna Caravan Professional package for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, offering either the passenger or amphibian & cargo variants separately or a full package with both. The Cessna C208 Caravan is widely known as the “Swiss Army Knife” of the aviation world, redefining the concept of a utility aircraft since its launch in 1982. It was designed to be a “next generation” bush plane, built for durability and simplicity, with a high-wing design, fixed tricycle landing gear, and a boxy fuselage that can be easily configured to carry passengers or cargo (and sometimes both). By opting for a turboprop engine (Pratt & Whitney PT6A) instead of a piston engine, the aircraft’s reliability was significantly increased, along with a significant boost in “hot and high” performance, making it very useful for remote environments around the world. It can be found in every corner of the world, be it shipping packages in America with FedEx (greatly responsible for its success, by the way), connecting remote villages in Africa, operating as an amphibian airplane in Asia, running missions, connecting regional airports in South America, and much more. Black Square claims it’s one of the most technically advanced airplanes ever developed for Microsoft Flight Simulator, featuring over 100 potential failures, 12 hot-swappable radio configurations, an advanced turbine engine simulation with beta range implementation, and the most advanced cabin temperature simulation in MSFS. Their EFB allows you to configure options, manage payload, control failures, monitor engines, electrical schematics, and environmental control systems, all from within the simulator. The built-in failure system supports persistent wear, MTBF, and scheduled failures for nearly every component, and its gauges are affected by physics, becoming stuck without engine vibration, just like in real life. The radio navigation systems available span multiple eras of the Caravan’s history, featuring a Bendix-King KNS-80 RNAV system, a Garmin GTN 750/650 (PMS50 or TDS), and a variety of radios, autopilot systems, and a weather radar. Furthermore, it also includes a 145+ page manual with instructions on all equipment, and 42 in-game checklists with control highlighting to aid beginners. It’s available on Just Flight’s website for $47.99 each (cargo / passenger) or $64.99 for the complete bundle. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates A350 Airliner for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has released v1.2.4 for its A350 Airliner, bringing a smaller, but fairly practical update, focused mainly on flight logic, navigation, and handling fixes, plus one notable addition to the EFB. On the systems side, the update fixes an issue where the aircraft could incorrectly enter descent mode and then refuse to return to climb mode, along with a bug that caused multiple planned step climbs to overlap on the position report page. iniBuilds has also corrected inaccurate fuel burn predictions in the MCDU, fixed DME stations displaying as VORs, and resolved unrealistic ADS-B vertical speed readouts. There are also a couple of flight handling improvements in here. According to iniBuilds, premature auto-rotation has been fixed, while roll axis sensitivity has been adjusted based on user feedback. Over on the EFB side, v1.2.4 adds an in-flight maintenance override, allowing users to repair maintenance items while airborne, which should be a useful quality-of-life option, especially during longer sectors. ‍ V1.2.4SYSTEMS ⚙️FIXED Aircraft had inadvertently entered descent mode and it was impossible to re-enter climb modeFIXED Having multiple planned step climbs causes them all to overlap on the position report pageFIXED Fuel burn predictions in MCDU are inaccurateFIXED Aircraft auto rotating prior to v1FIXED DMEs display as VORsFIXED ADS-B readout shows unrealistically high vertical speedsFIXED Adjusted roll axis sensitivity based on user feedback. EFB📱ADDED Override for maintenance items to be repaired in-flight The update is available now through the iniManager. For new customers, the A350 can be purchased on the iniBuilds Store, for around $80.04 USD. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds TriStar Releasing in March for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has now confirmed that its upcoming TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024 will release later this month, with the aircraft set to launch as a Microsoft Flight Simulator Marketplace exclusive for both PC and Xbox users. The news was shared during the recent first look livestream, which is currently available to watch on Microsoft’s official Twitch channel, and comes shortly after the developer published another, nearly 50-minute preview video, offering an overview of the aircraft’s systems, cockpit, and overall operation. The preview content has highlighted everything from the RB211 engines and S-duct configuration to the flight engineer panel, INS navigation, AFCS modes, and performance management systems, alongside full flight sequences covering startup, departure, cruise, and landing. The decision to launch as a Marketplace exclusive at release mirrors a similar approach taken with the A340 Airliner, meaning access will initially be limited to Microsoft’s in-sim storefront, before potentially expanding elsewhere at a later stage. We’ll, of course, follow the anticipated aircraft, leading up to release. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: VFRScenery Releases Walkaround V2 for X-Plane 11/12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. VFRScenery has recently released Walkaround V2 for X-Plane 11 and 12, now accompanied by a follow-up update, v2.3.2. This new version is a full rebuild of the legacy product, now written entirely in native C++, resulting in improved performance, better stability, and smoother transitions throughout. The core concept remains, letting users move freely around their aircraft, but V2 expands this significantly, most notably with a “WalkCabin” mode, allowing the user to transition naturally from exterior to interior. Now included is dynamic audio, including footsteps, environment-based sounds, and pilot/co-pilot dialogue tied to different flight phases. There’s also an integrated landing rate system that displays touchdown data such as vertical speed and G-force, with appropriate commentary. Shortly after release, VFRScenery rolled out v2.3.2, improving stair handling and ground-to-cabin transitions, with added support for double-deck aircraft. Additional fixes address camera positioning issues, while a new door height recording tool aims to improve aircraft compatibility going forward. Walkaround V2 is available now for XP11 and XP12, priced at $19.90 during the introductory period, and is free for existing Version 1 users. You can purchase it through the X-Plane.org Store. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: MK Studios Previews New Porto Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. MK Studios has shared the first preview images of its upcoming Porto Airport (LPPR) for MSFS 2024, revealing a complete rebuild of the scenery, and offering an early look at what is shaping up to be a significantly improved version. The developer said the project has now reached a stage where it feels ready to be shown, giving the community a glimpse at this new version of Porto, which is being built entirely from scratch. A highlight this time around is the use of high-resolution elevation data, allowing MK Studios to properly capture Porto’s distinctive terrain profile, something that has historically been difficult to replicate accurately. The new Porto Airport for MSFS 2024 is currently scheduled for release in April, with further previews and details expected in the lead-up to launch. View the full update post here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: New Video Showcases EFB for iFly 737 MAX 8 SP1
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. A new preview video from YouTube channel Wheels Up Live has offered us a closer look at the upcoming EFB overhaul for the iFly 737 MAX 8, as iFly lifts its preview embargo. The video was published by an iFly Discord moderator and beta tester, who makes clear throughout that everything shown remains work in progress. The preview focuses on the new tablet-style EFB, which replaces the older layout with a cleaner, more direct interface. Among the features showcased are panel state saving and loading, revised settings pages, manual links, cabin announcements, updated ground services and door controls, SimBrief-linked payload and balance, Navigraph integration, performance tools, failure reporting, display colour customisation, and a new 4x acceleration option. The presenter also shows support for using the EFB on an external iPad. Alongside the video, iFly has confirmed that beta testers are now working with the second SP1 beta, and are allowed to share videos, screenshots, and other previews. There is still no official ETA for release, and the features shown in the current beta may still change before launch. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Blackbird Simulations Releases C-130J Version 1.0.0 for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Blackbird Simulations has released version 1.0.0 of its C-130J for MSFS 2024, touching nearly every system onboard. The update brings a quite substantial list of fixes and refinements, including updates to the flight model, avionics logic, and overall aircraft behaviour, alongside improvements to performance and stability across the board. TCAS has been improved, as well as synchronised animations and effects, including doors, lighting, and various external elements. Sound has also been refined, with tweaks to engine loops, startup sequences, APU behaviour, and environmental effects. On the systems side, Blackbird has addressed a number of navigation and avionics issues, including fixes to airway handling, runway detection, and multiple bugs within the CNI-SP and navigation logic. Fuel system behaviour has been further refined, with more realistic transfer logic, updated pressure calculations, and improved handling of crossfeed and tank limits. The update also introduces various usability improvements, including adjustments to controls, tablet functionality, and interface clarity, while also addressing numerous smaller issues across panels, displays, among others. According to the developer, this release candidate represents a near-final version of the aircraft, with the full public release expected to follow shortly. Read the full changelog here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Aerosoft Releases Stuttgart Airport Trailer for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has released a trailer for its upcoming Stuttgart Airport (EDDS) for MSFS 2024, headed by the experienced scenery developers Jo Erlend Sund and Lukas Vezyroglou. The new rendition of Stuttgart aims to deliver a highly detailed and immersive experience, built exclusively for MSFS 2024, and taking full advantage of the platform’s latest SDK features. According to Aerosoft, the airport features an up-to-date layout as of March 2026, accurate modelling of buildings and infrastructure, and a custom terrain mesh that captures the airport’s distinct elevation profile. High-resolution textures, realistic night lighting, and custom taxiway signage and lighting are also included. Beyond the core airport, the scenery also includes several nearby landmarks, such as the Stuttgarter Fernsehturm, as well as the Altbach/Deizisau power plant, both featuring custom modelling and lighting effects. The airport environment includes animated interiors, walkable terminal areas in avatar mode, interactive elements, such as elevators and automatic doors, and custom vehicle traffic across the apron. Additional features include custom jetways, GSX integration with dedicated profiles, VDGS support, and AI traffic compatibility with airline-specific parking codes. Aerosoft has not yet confirmed a release date, but notes that Stuttgart Airport is “coming soon” to MSFS 2024. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!
